MCAR_uniform.mechanism_description: Each observation has an independent and equal probability of being missing. No variable influences missingness.
MCAR_uniform.structural_pattern: Pure binomial distribution — similar to flipping a coin per row
MCAR_uniform.expected_statistics: No mask-Xi correlation, uniform missing rate across X0 quartiles, no induced asymmetry

MCAR_fixed.mechanism_description: Exactly N randomly selected rows are missing. The total count is fixed, but the selection is uniform.
MCAR_fixed.structural_pattern: Sampling without replacement — guarantees exactly rate% missing
MCAR_fixed.expected_statistics: Identical to uniform, but with no variance in the total missing rate

MCAR_block.mechanism_description: Missing occurs in contiguous blocks (like temporal sensor failure). The block locations are random.
MCAR_block.structural_pattern: Temporal clusters of missing — long NaN runs interspersed with complete observations
MCAR_block.expected_statistics: Low mask entropy (repetitive pattern), but no correlation with Xi values

MAR_logistic.mechanism_description: P(missing|X1) follows a logistic function. Higher X1 values progressively increase the chance of X0 being missing.
MAR_logistic.expected_statistics: Positive mask-X1 correlation, significant X1_mean_diff, high AUC mask~X1

MAR_threshold.mechanism_description: X0 is missing when X1 exceeds a fixed threshold (1-rate quantile of X1). Deterministic mechanism.
MAR_threshold.structural_pattern: Abrupt cutoff — all with high X1 lose X0
MAR_threshold.expected_statistics: Very strong mask-X1 correlation, maximum X1_mean_diff, near-perfect separation

MAR_rank.mechanism_description: The N observations with the highest X1 values have X0 missing. Similar to threshold but rank-based.
MAR_rank.expected_statistics: Similar to threshold, strong unidirectional X1 dependence

MAR_quantile_group.mechanism_description: Missing probability varies by X1 quartile: Q1=20%, Q2=60%, Q3=140%, Q4=200% of base rate.
MAR_quantile_group.structural_pattern: Increasing probability staircase by X1 group
MAR_quantile_group.expected_statistics: Moderate mask-X1 correlation, unequal missing rate across X1 groups

MAR_multi_predictor.mechanism_description: P(missing|X1,X2) = sigmoid(0.7*norm(X1) + 0.3*norm(X2)). Depends on two variables with different weights.
MAR_multi_predictor.structural_pattern: Weighted dependence on two variables, X1 dominant
MAR_multi_predictor.expected_statistics: Correlation with X1 and X2, AUC mask~(X1,X2) higher than mask~X1 alone

MNAR_self_logistic.mechanism_description: P(missing|X0) follows a logistic function over X0. Higher X0 values are more likely to be missing.
MNAR_self_logistic.structural_pattern: Smooth self-censoring — the data with the most extreme values are the most likely to be missing
MNAR_self_logistic.expected_statistics: Biased observed X0 (negative skew from upper tail loss), increasing missing rate in upper quartiles, NO significant X1 correlation

MNAR_quantile_threshold.mechanism_description: X0 is missing when X0 >= quantile(1-rate). Deterministic truncation at the upper tail.
MNAR_quantile_threshold.structural_pattern: Abrupt cutoff at X0 value — upper tail completely removed
MNAR_quantile_threshold.expected_statistics: Strong negative skewness in observed X0, abnormal kurtosis, missing concentrated 100% in Q4

MNAR_tail_censoring.mechanism_description: Missing in the rate/2% lowest AND rate/2% highest X0 values. Symmetric bilateral censoring.
MNAR_tail_censoring.structural_pattern: Both tails removed — observed X0 is truncated at both extremes
MNAR_tail_censoring.expected_statistics: Reduced kurtosis (flatter distribution), narrower IQR than expected, missing in both Q1 and Q4

MNAR_self_noisy.mechanism_description: Similar to self_logistic, but with Gaussian noise N(0, 0.1) added to X0 before computing P(missing). Blurred mechanism.
MNAR_self_noisy.structural_pattern: Self-censoring with uncertainty — the missing threshold is fuzzy/diffuse
MNAR_self_noisy.expected_statistics: Similar to self_logistic but with softer and more diluted effects due to noise

_distribution_cycle.description: Base distributions cycle in order: uniform, normal, exponential, beta
